channel.title: tiling wm on earnest's site
channel.description: Recent content in tiling wm on earnest's site

item.title: Workspacer + Tiling Window Managers on Windows 10
item.description: Tiling Window Managers If you’re familiar with Linux operating systems, you may have used one or seen a tiling window manager at some point. If you haven’t, according to Wikipedia, a tiling window manager is a window manager with an organization of the screen into mutually non-overlapping frames, as opposed to the more popular approach of coordinate-based stacking of overlapping objects (windows) that tries to fully emulate the desktop metaphor.
